title: For investors with 1- to 2-year perspective and ability to ignore volatility: 6 stocks with an upside potential of up to 26%
summary: The US-Iran war is escalating again, with all that means for crude prices and nervous markets. And halfway across the world, the long AI trade is unwinding, dragging down the high-priced names that led the rally. Between the two, volatility, which has been the defining feature of 2026, is set to rise again. But volatility has a shelf life. It comes, it frightens, and it goes. What survives it is the underlying business, the quality of the management running it, and their proven ability to handle exactly this kind of complexity.

title: Have metal stocks’ dependence moved from Chinese apartments to world's power grid? 5 metal stocks with upside potential of up to 34%
summary: Weak Chinese growth has traditionally been a warning signal for metal stocks. Yet, the latest disappointing data from China has caused only a limited reaction, or literally nothing. It can be because of two reasons: Either the old rule is changing, or the bad news has been discounted by the market. Either way, it is time to relook at metal stocks, but only by investors who have the ability to be contrarian as well as tactical. Coming back to China: Has it stopped mattering? Not quite. It has started mattering in a more complicated way. And that is where the AI connection for metal starts.

title: After a decade of falling behind, UTI AMC begins its turnaround under a new chief
summary: Subramaniam, who took charge in February ahead of the annual planning cycle, likens his career to football—having progressed from team captain as head of equities to manager as chief investment officer, and now to running the club as chief executive.

title: A viral SIP myth is fooling investors. Here's why
summary: A viral post claims SIPs delivered just 6.7% over two decades. But the calculation is flawed—and the flat market it cites is precisely when SIPs quietly do their best work.

title: F&O Talk: Nifty IT gaining strong momentum, says Sudeep Shah; outlines HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank strategy after Q1
summary: The Nifty IT index has rebounded nearly 13.5% from its July 1 low and gained around 5% this week, signalling improving momentum. Technical indicators, including the RSI, MACD and Relative Rotation Graph (RRG), point to strengthening bullish sentiment. The 29,600–29,650 zone is the key resistance, while 28,500–28,550 is expected to act as immediate support.

title: Will Sensex, Nifty extend gains on Monday? Q1 earnings, global tech selloff among 6 factors to steer D-St this week
summary: Dalal Street ended the week on a strong note, with the Sensex and Nifty gaining over 1% on Friday. Investors will track six key market triggers this week, including the peak of the Q1 earnings season, the escalating Iran-US conflict, surging crude oil prices, the global tech selloff, rupee movement and FII flows. Analysts remain cautiously optimistic on market sentiment.

title: Retirement planning: Why the rule of 1% upgrade works better than a 1% increase in SIP amount
summary: The rule of 1% upgrade encourages investors to increase the share of their income invested by one percentage point every year. A small annual increase in the share of income invested can significantly boost long-term retirement savings.

title: EPF Scheme 2026: Why you must keep 25% of your PF balance before retirement
summary: The new EPF Scheme introduces a 25% minimum balance requirement for partial withdrawals. Here's what the rule means, how eligible member balance is calculated, and when you can withdraw your full PF corpus.

title: Sold house for  ₹1.10 crore, tax computed on  ₹1.96 crore? ITAT explains when Section 54 relief is still available
summary: The Pune ITAT has ruled that capital gains computed using the higher stamp duty value under Section 50C do not automatically deny Section 54 exemption. The tax benefit, however, is available only if the taxpayer satisfies all conditions for claiming the exemption.

title: Health insurance: 63% policyholders unable to switch from their plan — Here's why and key things to keep in mind
summary: An analysis by CoverSure showed that besides under-penetration of health insurance, another growing problem for many policyholders is that coverage exists but as many as 63% are unable to switch plans.

title: Monsoon Home Insurance: Focus on fine print, cheap premiums could cost you dearly
summary: There are many exclusions in your home insurance policy, and the policyholder must carefully review them to avoid being caught off guard at the worst possible moment.

title: Salary slip shows TDS deducted, but Form 26AS doesn't? Here's how the ITAT ruling protected the taxpayer
summary: An ITAT ruling reinforces that salaried employees cannot be asked to pay tax twice if their employer deducts TDS but fails to deposit it. The decision strengthens taxpayer protection and serves as a timely reminder to verify TDS records before filing income tax returns.
